name: World Bank MCP Server
what_it_does: World Bank MCP server providing access to the World Bank's open data APIs — querying economic indicators, development statistics, country data, and global development metrics from the World Bank's comprehensive data catalog. Enables AI agents to access authoritative global economic, social, and development data for research, analysis, and policy work.
use_cases: Querying GDP, poverty, and economic development indicators from research agents
Comparing country-level development metrics from policy analysis agents
Accessing historical economic data for trend analysis from economic research agents
Supporting international development and sustainability research from academic agents
Providing global economic context for business intelligence from market research agents
not_for: Real-time financial market data (World Bank data is development statistics with publication delays)
Sub-national or city-level data (World Bank data is primarily country and regional level)
Private sector financial data (World Bank focuses on public sector and development metrics)
best_when: A research or policy agent needs authoritative global economic and development data — World Bank has one of the most comprehensive freely-available datasets for international development indicators.
avoid_when: You need real-time economic data or financial market indicators — use Bloomberg, Reuters, or World Bank's paid data services for those.

auth.notes: No authentication required — World Bank data API is freely accessible. No API key needed for standard data access.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free and open access to all World Bank public data
pricing.notes: World Bank Open Data API is completely free. MCP server is free open source.

performance.rate_limits: World Bank API has rate limits for high-volume queries; generally generous

known_agent_gotchas: World Bank indicator codes are arcane (e.g., 'NY.GDP.MKTP.CD' for GDP) — agents need code lookup
Data has publication delays — some indicators are 1-2 years behind current year
Country codes use ISO 3-letter format — ensure correct country code mapping
Large indicator queries can return paginated results — implement pagination handling
Community MCP — verify which World Bank API version and endpoints are supported
Data coverage varies by indicator and country — some countries have missing data
agent_readiness.security_notes: Public data. No credentials. World Bank servers. No sensitive data involved. HTTPS. Queries public development statistics.

security_score_components.security_notes: Public data. No credentials needed. No sensitive data. World Bank open data API.
